title: Replicate gas UTXOs and checkpoints across peers
markdown:
## Goal

Design and implement the mechanism for replicating gas UTXOs and checkpoint records across multiple B3nd nodes. When a gas transaction is validated on one node, it must propagate to all peers so they have a consistent view of the unspent set. Checkpoints are compared across nodes to detect divergence. This is the foundation for multi-node gas and the prerequisite for cross-node double-spend detection.

## Background

Today, each B3nd node operates independently. The confirmation protocol (Group 2 work) provides the basic mechanism for nodes to attest to messages. Gas replication builds on this:

1. Gas transactions are replicated like any other message (via the peer replication mechanism from Group 2)
2. Checkpoints are shared between nodes: each validator node produces its own checkpoint chain
3. Nodes compare checkpoint hashes to detect divergence: if node A and node B have different checkpoints for the same window, something is wrong

The replication strategy must handle:
- **Ordering**: Gas UTXOs have dependencies (spend requires the UTXO to exist). Replication must respect this.
- **Conflict**: Two nodes could accept conflicting spends of the same UTXO. Checkpoint comparison detects this.
- **Bootstrapping**: A new node joining must sync the full UTXO set from peers.

This story focuses on the design document and a basic implementation that works with the existing `connect()` / peer mechanisms.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Design document: `docs/book/consensus/gas-replication.md` covering replication strategy, ordering guarantees, conflict detection, and bootstrap procedure
- [ ] `GasReplicator` class: `constructor(localNode, peerNodes, tracker, checkpointProducer)`
- [ ] `syncFromPeer(peerNode)` -- pulls gas UTXOs and checkpoints from a peer, validates, and writes to local node
- [ ] `pushToPeers(message)` -- after local validation, pushes gas-relevant messages to peers
- [ ] `compareCheckpoints(localCheckpoint, peerCheckpoint)` -- returns `{ match: boolean, divergence?: { sequence, localHash, peerHash } }`
- [ ] Replication respects UTXO ordering: consumed markers only replicated after the referenced UTXO exists locally
- [ ] Test: replicate gas genesis from node A to node B
- [ ] Test: replicate gas spend from node A to node B (with ordering)
- [ ] Test: checkpoint comparison detects matching checkpoints
- [ ] Test: checkpoint comparison detects divergence

## Implementation Guide

1. Read `libs/b3nd-listener/mod.ts` to understand the existing connect/poll replication pattern
2. Read `libs/firecat-protocol/gas-tracker.ts` for UTXO tracker
3. Read `libs/firecat-protocol/gas-checkpoint.ts` for checkpoint producer
4. Write design document first: `docs/book/consensus/gas-replication.md`
   - Cover: replication topology (gossip vs direct peer), message ordering, conflict detection via checkpoints, bootstrap sync
5. Create `libs/firecat-protocol/gas-replication.ts`:
   - `GasReplicator` wraps existing peer communication (HttpClient or similar)
   - On local gas message acceptance: push to peers
   - Periodically pull from peers: scan for new gas:// URIs
   - After each checkpoint: compare with peer checkpoints
6. For ordering: use a simple queue -- if a consumed marker references a UTXO we don't have, queue it and retry after syncing the UTXO
7. For checkpoint comparison: read `confirmation://checkpoints/{peerKey}/{sequence}` from peer node, compare hash with local checkpoint at same sequence
8. Create test with two MemoryClient nodes simulating peer replication

## Files to Create/Modify

- `docs/book/consensus/gas-replication.md` -- new design document
- `libs/firecat-protocol/gas-replication.ts` -- new file: GasReplicator class
- `libs/firecat-protocol/gas-replication_test.ts` -- new file: test suite

## Testing

```bash
deno test --allow-all libs/firecat-protocol/gas-replication_test.ts
```

## Dependencies

- **Depends on**: G3-B2-01 (UTXO tracker), G3-B2-03 (checkpoints)
- **Blocks**: G3-B3-02 (cross-node consumption detection), G3-B3-03 (relay proofs)

- [ ] Implement relay acknowledgment (`gas://relay-acks`)
- [ ] Implement operator staking (`gas://staking/{pubkey}` with sqrt-weighted rewards)
- [ ] Implement stratified validation (validators declare URI path ranges in staking message)
